framework.name: OAuth Scope Parameter Classification
framework.description: OAuth scopes divide into two categories: universally accepted parameters (standardized like 'openid', 'profile', 'email') and specialized parameters (custom-defined by service owners like 'read:posts', 'write:messages'). This dual structure enables both interoperability and service-specific customization.
framework.raw_quote: Universally Accepted Scope Parameters: Generally renowned presets that typically find their application in an array of OAuth integrations. 'openid', 'profile', 'email', 'address', and 'phone' are among the well-known ones. Specialized Scope Parameters: These elements are generated by the resource server or service owner and are typically unique to the individual software or service.
